affordances.name: Outside-view reference-class anchor before case-specific updating
affordances.mechanism: Start a forecast, diagnosis, budget, or estimate from the historical frequency of a structurally similar reference class, test that class for fit, then update with the specific facts instead of letting the inside-view story set the baseline.

use_when: A forecast, diagnosis, budget, or estimate is vulnerable to a vivid inside-view story.
A credible reference class exists for comparing the current case to similar past outcomes.
The decision depends on likely ranges or tail outcomes rather than a single optimistic base case.
do_not_use_when: The proposed reference class is too broad, too narrow, or structurally mismatched.
A real regime shift or causal break makes old frequencies an unsafe baseline.
The prior is being used to avoid updating on high-quality specific evidence.
case_evidence_needed: The outcome being forecast, diagnosed, budgeted, or estimated.
A named reference class of structurally similar past projects, outcomes, or cases.
Historical frequency, success/failure rates, likely ranges, or tail-outcome data for that class.
Case-specific evidence that may justify updating the outside-view prior.

treatment_requirements.description: Name the reference class, collect its historical frequency or success/failure rate, and test whether its structural assumptions match the current case before using it as the prior.
evidence_required: A proposed reference class of similar past problems or outcomes.
Aggregate historical data from that class.
The assumptions that make the current case comparable to the class.
Any specific facts that should update the prior.
good_output_shape: States the outside-view prior before the inside-view narrative.
Explains why the reference class fits or where fit is uncertain.
Updates the prior with specific evidence instead of discarding the prior.
Names any reference-class mismatch or regime shift that blocks promotion.

diagnostic_questions: What is the average success/failure rate for projects or situations like this, regardless of our unique details?
Do the underlying structures and core assumptions of this reference group actually match our current situation, or are we confusing familiarity with fit?
If a believable expert saw only the statistics and not the personal narrative, what would they say is the most likely outcome?
misuse_guards: Do not use a base rate when the chosen reference class is too broad, too narrow, or structurally mismatched.
Do not treat old frequencies as decisive when the case contains a real regime shift or causal break.
Do not let vivid, individualized, or emotionally salient details replace the general frequency of the outcome.

source_evidence.source_quote: The fundamental essence of the Base Rate model is the recognition and application of **prior probabilities** or **robust statistical regularities** when evaluating a specific case or making a prediction.
For sound reasoning and effective decisions, one should always seek to anchor first to this outside view.
a group of similar problems or outcomes from the past
The aggregate historical data from this class provides the base rate, setting the expectation for the current situation.
incorporating the prior probability (the base rate) before integrating the new, specific evidence.
a forecast, diagnosis, budget, or estimate is vulnerable to a vivid inside-view story that could drown out historical frequency.
a credible reference class exists and the team can compare the current case to that class before arguing from specialness.
the decision depends on calibrating likely ranges and tail outcomes rather than relying on a single optimistic base case.
the chosen reference class is too broad, too narrow, or structurally mismatched, making the prior look rigorous while actually misleading the decision.
the context contains a real regime shift or causal break, but the team still treats old frequencies as if nothing important has changed.
Systematically start the analysis by collecting and prioritizing the general statistics related to the outcome before incorporating specific facts.
Line up all the assumptions supporting the chosen reference class and rigorously test the current case for fit with each assumption.
What is the average success/failure rate for projects/situations like this, regardless of our unique details?
Do the underlying structures and core assumptions of this reference group actually match our current situation, or are we confusing familiarity with fit?
If a believable expert, viewing only the statistics and not my personal narrative, were to assess this, what would they say is the most likely outcome?
Ignoring the general frequency of an outcome because specific case details are compelling, vivid, or emotionally salient.

absence_records.reason: The source offers a single advertising example about demographic anxiety, but it does not supply a reusable treatment requirement beyond the already extracted reference-class anchoring mechanism.

source_evidence.source_quote: Understanding base rates can guide messaging strategy. If a demographic has a **higher base rate of anxiety** about a topic (like financial hardship), focusing on their deepest fears (avoiding loss) will resonate more than focusing solely on the benefits.
source_evidence.section_hint: Concrete Examples in Business and Strategy:

absence_records.reason: The source names System 2, motivated reasoning, cognitive entrenchment, scientific method, and humility as allies or failure modes for applying base rates, but they do not create a separate Base Rates transaction. The operational contract remains outside-view reference-class anchoring, with separate records owning deliberation, debiasing, and intellectual humility.

source_evidence.source_quote: Applying base rates is a process that requires effort, logic, and deliberation, classifying it as a **System 2** function
This desire-driven process actively resists the often inconvenient base rate.

absence_records.reason: The source supports historical frequencies only after reference-class fit survives a causal-break check. A base rate should not be promoted when a real regime shift makes old frequencies an unsafe prior.

source_evidence.source_quote: the context contains a real regime shift or causal break, but the team still treats old frequencies as if nothing important has changed.
For sound reasoning and effective decisions, one should always seek to anchor first to this outside view.
The aggregate historical data from this class provides the base rate, setting the expectation for the current situation.
Systematically start the analysis by collecting and prioritizing the general statistics related to the outcome before incorporating specific facts.
Line up all the assumptions supporting the chosen reference class and rigorously test the current case for fit with each assumption.

review_notes.normalization_note: Merged the raw outside-view principle, reference-class forecasting playbook, Wave 1 activation context, and Wave 2 intervention semantics into one operational affordance. Wave 3 relation material was read as context but not promoted into a separate affordance.
dropped_material: Did not extract separate affordances for System 1, System 2, the scientific method, motivated reasoning, or cognitive entrenchment because the operational demand is already represented by the outside-view reference-class treatment.
Did not promote the advertising and messaging example because it is domain-specific application context rather than a general operational requirement.
open_questions: Future runtime evaluation should decide whether relation-only material belongs in model-affordance records or remains in relation semantics.
